project.name: Pathway
project.goal: Portfolio proof project for a Senior React Native, Expo, React and Next.js engineer.

milestones.title: Foundation and Seeded Content
evidence: pnpm workspace with apps/mobile, apps/web, apps/cms, packages/api, packages/ui-tokens
Workspace package names: @pathway/mobile, @pathway/web, @pathway/cms, @pathway/api, @pathway/ui-tokens
Expo, Next.js, and Strapi scaffolds present and runnable
Strapi content types defined: Learning Path, Module, Lesson, Category, Author
Seed script apps/cms/scripts/seed-pathway.js creates 4 categories, 1 author, 4 learning paths, 12 modules, 36 lessons
SQLite database apps/cms/.tmp/data.db contains 5 learning_paths (1 published: React Native Performance), 12 modules, 45 lessons (9 published), 4 categories, 1 author
1 admin user exists in the database
Public read permissions configured for learning-path, module, lesson, category, author
docs/architecture.md documents the three-surface split
docs/cms-seeding.md documents the reset-and-seed workflow

milestones.title: Shared CMS-to-Client Vertical Slice
milestones.goal: Prove that Strapi is the single published content source used by both Expo and Next.js.
acceptanceCriteria: A published title changed in Strapi appears in Expo after refresh.
A published title changed in Strapi appears in Next.js after refresh.
Both clients consume the shared API package.
External CMS data is validated at the API boundary.
No production content is hardcoded independently in mobile or web.
evidence: packages/api exports createPathwayApiClient, LearningPath, LearningPathModule, LessonPreview, ContentImage, Difficulty, resolveStrapiMediaUrl, PathwayApiValidationError, PathwayApiHttpError, PathwayApiNetworkError
packages/api/src/strapi/learning-path.schema.ts contains Zod schemas validating Strapi responses at runtime
packages/api/src/strapi/learning-path.mapper.ts maps Strapi payloads to camelCase domain models
packages/api/src/domain/learning-path.ts defines typed domain models: LearningPath, LearningPathModule, LessonPreview, ContentImage, Difficulty
pnpm --filter @pathway/api test passes 23 tests (parser, mapper, media URL resolver)
pnpm --filter @pathway/api exec tsc --noEmit passes
apps/mobile/src/lib/pathway-api.ts creates singleton PathwayApiClient via createPathwayApiClient
apps/mobile/src/app/index.tsx fetches featured learning paths via api.getFeaturedLearningPaths with fallback to getPublishedLearningPaths
apps/mobile/src/components/learning-path-card.tsx renders LearningPath domain model fields only
apps/web/src/lib/pathway-api.ts creates server-only singleton PathwayApiClient
apps/web/src/app/(home)/page.tsx fetches featured learning paths and links cards to /paths/[slug]
apps/web/src/app/paths/[slug]/page.tsx fetches by slug via api.getLearningPathBySlug, calls notFound() for missing slug, generateMetadata derives title from real content
No /api/learning-paths, documentId, data.attributes, or attributes. references found in apps/web/src or apps/mobile/src
No production content titles hardcoded in apps/web/src or apps/mobile/src
No `any` types in packages/api/src, apps/web/src, or apps/mobile/src

Manual proof: changed Strapi title to 'React Native Performance — M1 Audit Sync Test', confirmed updated title in Next.js path page h1 and title tag, confirmed API returns updated title to both clients, restored original title
Strapi logs confirm both Next.js (slug filter) and Expo (featured filter) requests go through @pathway/api
Web 404: /paths/slug-que-nao-existe returns HTTP 404 with noindex meta tag
Web empty state: 'No learning paths have been published yet.' in apps/web/src/app/(home)/page.tsx
Mobile error/retry: 'Couldn’t load learning paths.' + 'Try again' button in apps/mobile/src/app/index.tsx
Commit 94243fa: feat(pathway): complete shared CMS vertical slice

milestones.title: Expo Mobile Core Journey

parts.title: Mobile foundation — design system, app shell, tabs e rotas
Mobile hardening — system states, accessibility, navigation review and milestone validation

evidence: Expo Router navigation shell with 4 tabs (Home, Explore, Saved, Profile) and 2 detail routes (/paths/[slug], /lessons/[slug]) in apps/mobile/src/app/

Neo-Academic Brutalist design system: Epilogue headings, Inter body, #FAF9F5 background, #000000 borders (3px primary, 2px thin), hard shadows, mint #D4E7DD, acid green #79FF5B, active green #38FE13, error #BA1A1A — all from shared @pathway/ui-tokens

Home screen (apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/index.tsx) loads featured learning paths via useFeaturedLearningPathsQuery, shows greeting, continue learning card with real progress, featured paths, recommended lessons, recently saved with hydration skeleton

Explore screen (apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/explore.tsx) loads all published paths via usePublishedLearningPathsQuery, client-side search and topic filters (All, Mobile, Accessibility, Product, AI), no-results state, loading skeleton, error retry

Learning Path Detail (apps/mobile/src/app/paths/[slug].tsx) loads by slug via useLearningPathBySlugQuery, shows hero with cover/metadata/progress/CTA, module accordions with lesson rows, bookmark toggle, back navigation

Lesson Detail (apps/mobile/src/app/lessons/[slug].tsx) loads by slug via useLessonBySlugQuery, shows context link, tags, title, summary, author, media preview, path progress, body renderer, key takeaway, completion card, previous/next navigation
Saved screen (apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/saved.tsx) with segmented control (Lessons/Paths), resolves saved slugs against published content, distinguishes empty vs unavailable, storage error notice

Profile screen (apps/mobile/src/app/(tabs)/profile.tsx) with hero, learning overview grid (completed lessons, saved items, active paths, completed paths), active/completed path cards, completed lesson rows, local data notice

Local persistence via AsyncStorage (apps/mobile/src/features/learning-activity/): versioned payload (@pathway/learning-activity:v1), type-guarded read, single-flight write queue, hydration before any writes, storage error handling without data loss
LearningActivityProvider wraps the app in _layout.tsx, persists saved lessons, saved paths, and completed lessons across app restarts
packages/api extended with getLessonBySlug, LessonDetail domain model (body, author, category, media), lesson Zod schema and mapper — 41 tests pass

Loading states: structural skeletons (HomeSkeleton, ExploreSkeleton, LearningPathDetailSkeleton, LessonDetailSkeleton, SavedContentSkeleton, ProfileSkeleton) with black borders, no shimmer/gradient, not a broken screen

Empty states: EmptyState component with icon variants (bookmark, warning, grid) — NOTHING SAVED YET, VOID DETECTED, NO SAVED PATHS, CURRICULUM COMING SOON, LESSON CONTENT UNAVAILABLE, NO ACTIVE PATHS, NO COMPLETED PATHS, NO COMPLETED LESSONS

Error states: ErrorState component with retry (real refetch) and optional secondary action (BACK TO EXPLORE) — user-facing messages from @pathway/api USER_FACING_MESSAGES, no stack trace or JSON shown
Unavailable content: distinct from empty (warning icon) — SAVED CONTENT UNAVAILABLE, preserves local slugs, does not delete user data, discrete notice when some items are unavailable

Hydration: no screen shows empty state or 0% progress before isHydrated; ContinueLearningCard and LearningPathHero show RESTORING PROGRESS; bookmark and completion buttons disabled during hydration with accessibilityState.disabled

Image fallbacks: PathCover component with onError fallback to geometric CoverFallback (cover and thumbnail variants), LessonMediaPreview with onError fallback to AbstractMediaFallback — no broken image areas

Accessibility: tab labels (Home tab, Explore tab, Saved tab, Profile tab), card labels (Open learning path/lesson {title}), bookmark labels (Save/Remove {title}), completion labels (Mark lesson {title} as complete/incomplete), navigation labels (Go to previous/next lesson {title}), module accordion expand/collapse with accessibilityState.expanded, decorative icons hidden
Safe areas: Screen component adds bottom safe area inset to bottomInset automatically, BottomTabBar respects safe area bottom, detail routes do not show bottom tab bar
No Pressables nested that both fire: Saved cards have separate content area and bookmark button, touching bookmark does not navigate
No `any` types in apps/mobile/src or packages/api/src
pnpm --filter @pathway/mobile exec tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm --filter @pathway/mobile lint passes
pnpm --filter @pathway/api test passes (41 tests, 0 fail)
pnpm --filter @pathway/api exec tsc --noEmit passes

CMS running and API verified: curl http://localhost:1337/api/learning-paths returns published React Native Performance path, curl API for lessons returns published Optimizing Long Lists with FlashList lesson
Expo app running in iOS simulator (pnpm dev:mobile:ios)
docs/milestone-2-smoke-test.md created with manual validation checklist
ADR-003-local-persistence-strategy.md records the AsyncStorage persistence decision

milestones.title: Next.js Public Web Experience

parts.title: Complete public Learning Path detail experience
Related learning paths and final quality pass
Public dynamic Lesson routes — shell, metadata, safe navigation
Complete editorial reading experience for lesson routes
Sharing controls, related lessons, previous/next navigation, final quality pass

evidence: Public routes: / (homepage), /explore, /paths/[slug], /lessons/[slug] — all server-rendered via @pathway/api
Homepage (apps/web/src/app/(public)/page.tsx): fetches featured paths via getHomepageData, HomeHero with real path links, revalidate=300
Explore (apps/web/src/app/(public)/explore/page.tsx): getExploreContent, client-side search + topic/difficulty filters, noindex on filtered variants, canonical to /explore

Learning Path route (apps/web/src/app/(public)/paths/[slug]/page.tsx): getPathDetailView via @pathway/api, notFound() for missing/unpublished, generateStaticParams, revalidate=300, full hero + curriculum + sticky summary + related paths

Lesson route (apps/web/src/app/(public)/lessons/[slug]/page.tsx): getLessonDetailView via @pathway/api, notFound() for missing/unpublished, generateStaticParams, revalidate=300, full hero + media + TOC + Markdown body renderer + share + prev/next nav + parent CTA + related lessons

Metadata: buildPathMetadata, buildLessonMetadata, buildCanonicalUrl in apps/web/src/lib/metadata.ts — title, description, canonical, OG (website/article), twitter card, article:published_time for lessons
404/not-found: root not-found.tsx + (public)/not-found.tsx with noindex, ContentState component with Back to home + Explore actions
Error boundary: error.tsx Client Component with toUserFacingError, USER_FACING_MESSAGES, retry + back to home
Sitemap (apps/web/src/app/sitemap.ts): dynamic, lists /, /explore, /paths/[slug], /lessons/[slug] from published content, revalidate=300
Robots (apps/web/src/app/robots.ts): allows /, /explore, /paths/, /lessons/, disallows /api/, /signin, /topics
On-demand revalidation: POST /api/revalidate route handler with Bearer auth (timingSafeEqual), parseWebhookPayload + routesToRevalidate, ADR-004 documents the strategy
ISR: revalidate=300 on all public content routes; on-demand webhook is an optimization with ISR fallback
Data layers: path-data.ts, lesson-data.ts, homepage-data.ts, explore-data.ts — all server-only, React cache() for request dedup, status discriminator (ok/missing/error)
Lesson body parser: lesson-body-parser.ts — typed Markdown → semantic HTML, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, 14 parser tests
Web lib tests: 70 tests pass (explore-filters 25, lesson-body-parser 14, lesson-navigation 12, related-paths 7, revalidation 12)
API tests: 47 tests pass
pnpm --filter @pathway/api exec tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm --filter @pathway/web exec tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm --filter @pathway/web lint passes
pnpm --filter @pathway/web build passes — /paths/[slug] and /lessons/[slug] are SSG (●), / and /explore are dynamic (ƒ), sitemap revalidate 5m
pnpm --filter @pathway/mobile exec tsc --noEmit passes (M3 did not break mobile)
No `any` types in apps/web/src (grep for ': any|as any|<any>' returns empty)
No raw Strapi payload interpretation in web components — all components receive typed view models from data layers
No hardcoded production content in web — all content from @pathway/api
No custom CMS admin, CRUD, or editorial dashboard in Next.js
No V2 features introduced (no auth, payments, analytics, sync, video player)
Manual demo (2026-07-06): Strapi CMS started on localhost:1337 with published React Native Performance path (3 modules, 9 published lessons); Next.js dev server on localhost:3001
Manual demo: GET / → 200, <title>Pathway</title>, meta description, canonical, OG tags, h1 'Build sharper product and engineering skills.'
Manual demo: GET /paths/react-native-performance → 200, <title>React Native Performance | Pathway</title>, meta description from Strapi, canonical, OG (type=website), h1 with real title

Manual demo: GET /lessons/optimizing-long-lists-with-flashlist → 200, <title>Optimizing Long Lists with FlashList | Pathway</title>, meta description from Strapi summary, canonical, OG (type=article), article:published_time, h1 with real title, article body with 3 h2 headings (Why FlashList, Key props, Common mistake), author Jonathan Ramalho with bio
Manual demo: GET /explore → 200, <title>Explore | Pathway</title>, meta description, canonical, h1 'Explore practical technical learning'
Manual demo: GET /paths/slug-que-nao-existe → not-found with noindex, <title>Learning path not found | Pathway</title>
Manual demo: GET /lessons/slug-que-nao-existe → not-found with noindex, <title>Lesson not found | Pathway</title>
Manual demo: GET /sitemap.xml → 200, lists /, /explore, /paths/react-native-performance, 9 lesson URLs
Manual demo: GET /robots.txt → 200, allows public routes, disallows /api/, /signin, /topics, points to sitemap
Manual demo: mobile User-Agent (iPhone) — all routes render with viewport meta and h1 content
Manual demo: path page links to lessons (/lessons/[slug]); lesson page links to parent path (/paths/[slug]) and related lessons
Manual demo: lesson article body contains real Strapi Markdown content rendered to semantic HTML (h2, p, code blocks)

Manual demo NOT executed: on-demand revalidation via Strapi webhook — could not obtain admin credentials or valid API token to trigger a content change; ISR (revalidate=300) and webhook endpoint are implemented and unit-tested (12 revalidation tests pass) but end-to-end webhook trigger was not validated manually
ADR-004-on-demand-revalidation.md records the cache, render, and revalidation strategy for public Next.js content

milestones.title: Product Polish and Engineering Signals

parts.title: Mobile discovery screens polish (Home, Explore, Saved)
Mobile learning flow polish (Path Detail, Lesson Detail)
Loading, error, empty, and unavailable states hardening
CI for lint, typecheck, and tests

evidence: M4.3: Learning Path Detail hero with contextual CTA (START PATH / CONTINUE LEARNING / REVIEW PATH) and human-language progress summary
M4.3: ModuleAccordion shows lesson count, completion count, and a checkmark when the module is complete
M4.3: LessonRow uses LessonStatusIndicator (icon + text pill) so lesson state never relies on color alone
M4.3: LessonStatusIndicator component (completed/current/upcoming/default) with icon + label + background tint
M4.3: Lesson Detail uses lesson.learningPath and lesson.module refs from the API for the context breadcrumb (no longer scans all paths)
M4.3: LessonContextLink shows a compact breadcrumb: path title › module title
M4.3: LessonBodyRenderer parses Markdown into typed blocks (headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, code, inline bold/italic/code/link) — mobile parser mirrors the web parser
M4.3: Lesson body reading layout with comfortable line-height, editorial spacing, dark code blocks, mint block quotes
M4.3: LessonCompletionCard shows inline path-completion feedback (PATH COMPLETE banner + REVIEW PATH CTA) when completing the last lesson
M4.3: LessonNavigation uses router.replace so navigating to prev/next lesson resets scroll to top
M4.3: All path-detail and lesson-detail components use @pathway/ui-tokens consistently (no hardcoded #000000/#FAF9F5/#D4E7DD)
M4.3: Skeletons updated to use tokens
M4.3: Mobile parser self-check test (8 tests) passes via node --test
M4.3: pnpm --filter @pathway/mobile exec tsc --noEmit passes
M4.3: pnpm --filter @pathway/mobile lint passes (0 errors, 0 warnings)
M4.3: pnpm --filter @pathway/api test passes (47 tests)
M4.3: pnpm --filter @pathway/web exec tsc --noEmit passes (M4.3 did not break web)
M4.3: pnpm --filter @pathway/web lint passes
M4.3: No `any` types introduced in apps/mobile/src

milestones.title: Web public page polish
evidence: M4.4: Homepage now renders real content sections below the hero: FeaturedPathsSection, PopularLessonsSection, TopicsSection, PracticalLearningSection — all driven by existing HomepageData (no new data layer)
M4.4: SectionHeader reusable component (eyebrow + title + supporting) for editorial rhythm across homepage sections
M4.4: FeaturedPathsSection reuses ExplorePathCard for visual consistency; links to real /paths/[slug]
M4.4: PopularLessonsSection renders compact lesson cards with parent path context, difficulty, duration — no fake popularity metrics
M4.4: TopicsSection renders real topic summaries derived from featured paths; each links to /explore (real discovery surface)
M4.4: PracticalLearningSection explains product positioning honestly on a forest-green surface — no corporate marketing or false career promises
M4.4: Homepage gracefully omits content sections when CMS is unavailable or empty (each section returns null on empty data); hero retains visual strength
M4.4: Focus-visible states added to all interactive elements in public shell: header wordmark, sign-in link, desktop nav links, mobile nav trigger + links, footer links, hero CTAs
M4.4: Explore, Path page, Lesson page already complete from M3 — no regression; visual consistency with mobile confirmed
M4.4: pnpm --filter @pathway/web exec tsc --noEmit passes
M4.4: pnpm --filter @pathway/web lint passes
M4.4: pnpm --filter @pathway/web build passes (21 static pages generated)
M4.4: pnpm --filter @pathway/web tests pass (70 tests)
M4.4: pnpm --filter @pathway/api test passes (47 tests)
M4.4: pnpm --filter @pathway/mobile exec tsc --noEmit passes (M4.4 did not break mobile)
M4.4: No `any` types introduced in apps/web/src
M4.4: No new dependencies added

invariants.rule: Strapi is the source of truth for structured content, media and publishing.
Expo owns the mobile learning product and Next.js owns public web discovery, SEO and shareable reading pages.
Next.js must not recreate Strapi admin or general content CRUD.
Do not introduce V2 features before the active milestone is demonstrably complete.
External CMS data must be runtime-validated at the API boundary.

nextTask.summary: Audit and consolidate the shared visual foundation across mobile and web, then implement the smallest set of reusable primitives needed to make the primary flows visually coherent and production-minded.
